title: All Whites suffer World Cup blow hours before first game: 'We are gutted'
original_text:
New Zealand attacker Matt Garbett will miss the 2026 FIFA World Cup due to a hamstring injury.

An 11th-hour injury blow, Garbett’s tournament is over before it began having picked up a hamstring problem in training.

New Zealand Football made the announcement just hours prior to the All Whites’ Group G opener against Iran at Los Angeles Stadium in Inglewood on Tuesday (AEST).

The Peterborough midfielder and 30-time international has been replaced by Logan Rogerson, who is fresh off winning the A-League Men Championship with Auckland FC.



“The whole squad’s thoughts are with Matt at this time and we are gutted he won’t be able to play in the tournament,” a statement read.

“We look forward to seeing him back on the pitch soon.”

Garbett’s absence is a blow for Darren Bazeley’s New Zealand, who are returning to the global stage for the first time in 16 years.

The 24-year-old had been expected to start against Iran.

He was coming off a three-goal, two-assist season for Peterborough in England’s third tier.

New Zealand, meanwhile, are in Group G in North America, alongside Iran, Belgium and Egypt.

Sacha Pisani Sacha Pisani is a sports journalist and content producer, who has covered domestic and international football - including the 2018 FIFA World Cup in Russia, with the A-Leagues and previously Stats Perform. Tags: Logan Rogerson Matt Garbett New Zealand Logan Rogerson Matt Garbett New Zealand
